human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1124 requirements, behavioral-health operations boundary discipline, SAMHSA CCBHC and crisis workflow grounding, CMS CMHC documentation controls, HHS HIPAA and Part 2 privacy controls, scenario coverage, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and cross-industry research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus cross-industry are represented and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and this readiness evidence.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to behavioral-health operations administration, including referral readiness, consent and ROI tracking, assessment and treatment-plan completeness, scheduling and crisis-transition coordination, authorization packaging, incident and grievance workflow packaging, and discharge evidence control without clinical or legal drift.

Authority sources cover SAMHSA CCBHC criteria, CMS CMHC surveyor guidance, SAMHSA crisis-care workflow, SAMHSA behavioral-health quality-measure evidence handling, and HHS HIPAA plus Part 2 privacy controls with refresh and decay metadata.

Scenario set covers missing industry evidence, evidence-free completion attempts, crisis-transition conflicts, privacy-sensitive disclosure requests, clinical-boundary rejections, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; service-model, state, payer, tenant workflow, and co-mingled SUD privacy differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
